title: cymbopetalum hintonii subsp. hintonii
intro: Cymbopetalum hintonii subsp. hintonii, also known as the "Mexican prickly poppy" or "Cabeza de ratón," is a plant species belonging to the family Annonaceae. It has been traditionally used in Mexican folk medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The plant's health benefits include its potential to reduce pain and inflammation, as well as its antioxidant properties, which can help protect against cell damage and oxidative stress. In herbal preparations, the plant's seeds are often used to make a tea or tincture, which can be used to treat various health conditions, including arthritis and digestive issues.
